VersionVector,\n    58→} from \"./types.js\";\n    59→import {\n    60→  MAX_OPERATION_ID,\n    61→  MIN_OPERATION_ID,\n    62→  compareOperationIds,\n    63→  operationIdsEqual,\n    64→  replicaId,\n    65→  transactionId,\n    66→} from \"./types.js\";\n    67→import { UndoMap } from \"./undo-map.js\";\n    68→\n    69→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    70→// Fragment sorting for canonical order\n    71→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    72→\n    73→/**\n    74→ * Compare locators for fragment sorting. This differs from lexicographic\n    75→ * comparison: when one locator is a prefix of another, we consider them\n    76→ * \"at the same position\" and return 0 to allow tie-breaking by operation ID.\n    77→ *\n    78→ * This ensures that when a fragment is split (getting child locators) and\n    79→ * another fragment has the same parent locator, they interleave correctly\n    80→ * based on operation ID, rather than the parent always sorting first.\n    81→ */\n    82→function compareLocatorsForSort(a: Locator, b: Locator): number {\n    83→  const minLen = Math.min(a.levels.length, b.levels.length);\n    84→\n    85→  // Compare common prefix\n    86→  for (let i = 0; i < minLen; i++) {\n    87→    const aLevel = a.levels[i];\n    88→    const bLevel = b.levels[i];\n    89→    if (aLevel !== undefined && bLevel !== undefined && aLevel !== bLevel) {\n    90→      return aLevel - bLevel;\n    91→    }\n    92→  }\n    93→\n    94→  // One is a prefix of the other (or they're equal).\n    95→  // Treat as \"same position\" to allow tie-breaking by operation ID.\n    96→  return 0;\n    97→}\n    98→\n    99→/**\n   100→ * Sort fragments to ensure canonical order regardless of operation application\n   101→ * sequence.\n   102→ *\n   103→ * Sort key: (locator prefix, insertionId, insertionOffset, locator length)\n   104→ *\n   105→ * This ensures:\n   106→ * 1. Fragments at the same position (locator prefix) sort by operation ID\n   107→ * 2. Split parts of the same operation sort by offset\n   108→ * 3. Child locators sort after parent locators with lower operation IDs\n   109→ */\n   110→function sortFragments(frags: Fragment[]): void {\n   111→  frags.sort((a, b) => {\n   112→    // First, compare by locator prefix\n   113→    const locCmp = compareLocatorsForSort(a.locator, b.locator);\n   114→    if (locCmp !== 0) return locCmp;\n   115→\n   116→    // Same prefix: tie-break by operation ID\n   117→    const idCmp = compareOperationIds(a.insertionId, b.insertionId);\n   118→    if (idCmp !== 0) return idCmp;\n   119→\n   120→    // Same operation: sort by insertionOffset (split parts)\n   121→    const offsetCmp = a.insertionOffset - b.insertionOffset;\n   122→    if (offsetCmp !== 0) return offsetCmp;\n   123→\n   124→    // Finally, sort by locator length (children after parent)\n   125→    return a.locator.levels.length - b.locator.levels.length;\n   126→  });\n   127→}\n   128→\n   129→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   130→// Transaction tracking\n   131→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   132→\n   133→interface Transaction {\n   134→  id: TransactionId;\n   135→  operationIds: OperationId[];\n   136→  timestamp: number;\n   137→}\n   138→\n   139→interface UndoEntry {\n   140→  transactionId: TransactionId;\n   141→  operationIds: OperationId[];\n   142→  /** The undo counts that were set. Needed for redo. */\n   143→  undoCounts: Array<{ operationId: OperationId; oldCount: number; newCount: number }>;\n   144→}\n   145→\n   146→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   147→// TextBuffer\n   148→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   149→\n   150→export class TextBuffer {\n   151→  private _replicaId: ReplicaId;\n   152→  private clock: LamportClock;\n   153→  private fragments: SumTree<Fragment, FragmentSummary>;\n   154→  private undoMap: UndoMap;\n   155→  private _version: VersionVector;\n   156→\n   157→  // Transaction tracking\n   158→  private nextTransactionId: number;\n   159→  private activeTransaction: Transaction | null;\n   160→  private transactionHistory: Transaction[];\n   161→\n   162→  // Time-based transaction grouping\n   163→  private groupDelay: number;\n   164→  private implicitTransaction: Transaction | null;\n   165→  private lastEditType: \"insert\" | \"delete\" | null;\n   166→\n   167→  // Undo/Redo stacks\n   168→  private undoStack: UndoEntry[];\n   169→  private redoStack: UndoEntry[];\n   170→\n   171→  // Track applied remote operation IDs for idempotency\n   172→  private appliedOps: Set<string>;\n   173→\n   174→  // Pending operations waiting for their causal dependencies\n   175→  private pendingOps: Operation[];\n   176→\n   177→  /** Injectable time source for testing. */\n   178→  private _now: () => number;\n   179→\n   180→  // Snapshot tracking for epoch-based reclamation\n   181→  private _liveSnapshots: number;\n   182→\n   183→  private constructor(rid: ReplicaId) {\n   184→    this._replicaId = rid;\n   185→    this.clock = new LamportClock(rid);\n   186→    this.fragments = new SumTree<Fragment, FragmentSummary>(fragmentSummaryOps);\n   187→    this.undoMap = new UndoMap();\n   188→    this._version = createVersionVector();\n   189→    this.nextTransactionId = 0;\n   190→    this.activeTransaction = null;\n   191→    this.transactionHistory = [];\n   192→    this.groupDelay = 300;\n   193→    this.implicitTransaction = null;\n   194→    this.lastEditType = null;\n   195→    this.undoStack = [];\n   196→    this.redoStack = [];\n   197→    this.appliedOps = new Set();\n   198→    this.pendingOps = [];\n   199→    this._now = Date.now;\n   200→    this._liveSnapshots = 0;\n   201→  }\n   202→\n   203→  /**\n   204→   * Create an empty TextBuffer.\n   205→   */\n   206→  static create(rid?: ReplicaId): TextBuffer {\n   207→    const r = rid ?? generateReplicaId();\n   208→    return new TextBuffer(r);\n   209→  }\n   210→\n   211→  /**\n   212→   * Create a TextBuffer initialized with the given text.\n   213→   * Uses a fast path that bypasses most CRDT infrastructure for initial content.\n   214→   */\n   215→  static fromString(text: string, rid?: ReplicaId): TextBuffer {\n   216→    const buffer = TextBuffer.create(rid);\n   217→    if (text.length > 0) {\n   218→      // Only normalize if \\r characters are present (rare case)\n   219→      const normalized = text.includes(\"\\r\")\n   220→        ? text.replace(/\\r\\n/g, \"\\n\").replace(/\\r/g, \"\\n\")\n   221→        : text;\n   222→\n   223→      // Fast path: create fragment directly without full CRDT insert machinery.\n   224→      // We still need: clock tick, version update, and undo tracking.\n   225→      const opId = buffer.clock.tick();\n   226→      observeVersion(buffer._version, buffer._replicaId, opId.counter);\n   227→\n   228→      // Record for undo support (initial content should be undoable)\n   229→      buffer.recordImplicitOp(opId, \"insert\");\n   230→\n   231→      // Use a simple locator between MIN and MAX\n   232→      const locator = locatorBetween(MIN_LOCATOR, MAX_LOCATOR);\n   233→      const fragment = createFragment(opId, 0, locator, normalized, true);\n   234→\n   235→      // Build SumTree with single fragment\n   236→      buffer.fragments = SumTree.fromItems([fragment], fragmentSummaryOps);\n   237→    }\n   238→    return buffer;\n   239→  }\n   240→\n   241→  get replicaId(): ReplicaId {\n   242→    return this._replicaId;\n   243→  }\n   244→\n   245→  get version(): VersionVector {\n   246→    return this._version;\n   247→  }\n   248→\n   249→  /**\n   250→   * Set the time-based grouping delay in milliseconds.\n   251→   * Consecutive same-type edits within this window are grouped as one undo unit.\n   252→   * Default is 300ms.\n   253→   */\n   254→  setGroupDelay(ms: number): void {\n   255→    this.groupDelay = ms;\n   256→  }\n   257→\n   258→  /**\n   259→   * Override the time source used for grouping (useful for tests).\n   260→   */\n   261→  setTimeSource(now: () => number): void {\n   262→    this._now = now;\n   263→  }\n   264→\n   265→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   266→  // Read API (convenience — prefer snapshot() for consistency)\n   267→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   268→\n   269→  /** Total UTF-16 length of visible text. */\n   270→  get length(): number {\n   271→    return this.fragments.summary().visibleLen;\n   272→  }\n   273→\n   274→  /** Get the visible text content. */\n   275→  getText(): string {\n   276→    const parts: string[] = [];\n   277→    for (const frag of this.fragmentsArray()) {\n   278→      if (frag.visible) {\n   279→        parts.push(frag.text);\n   280→      }\n   281→    }\n   282→    return parts.join(\"\");\n   283→  }\n   284→\n   285→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   286→  // Editing\n   287→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   288→\n   289→  /**\n   290→   * Insert text at the given visible UTF-16 offset.\n   291→   * Returns the Operation for collaboration broadcast.\n   292→   */\n   293→  insert(offset: number, text: string): Operation {\n   294→    if (text.length === 0) {\n   295→      const opId = this.clock.tick();\n   296→      observeVersion(this._version, this._replicaId, opId.counter);\n   297→      return {\n   298→        type: \"insert\",\n   299→        id: opId,\n   300→        text: \"\",\n   301→        after: { insertionId: MIN_OPERATION_ID, offset: 0 },\n   302→        before: { insertionId: MAX_OPERATION_ID, offset: 0 },\n   303→        version: cloneVersionVector(this._version),\n   304→        locator: MIN_LOCATOR,\n   305→      };\n   306→    }\n   307→\n   308→    const normalized = text.replace(/\\r\\n/g, \"\\n\").replace(/\\r/g, \"\\n\");\n   309→    return this.insertInternal(offset, normalized);\n   310→  }\n   311→\n   312→  /**\n   313→   * Delete text in the range [start, end).\n   314→   * Returns the Operation for collaboration broadcast.\n   315→   */\n   316→  delete(start: number, end: number): Operation {\n   317→    const clampedStart = Math.max(0, Math.min(start, this.length));\n   318→    const clampedEnd = Math.max(clampedStart, Math.min(end, this.length));\n   319→\n   320→    if (clampedStart === clampedEnd) {\n   321→      const opId = this.clock.tick();\n   322→      observeVersion(this._version, this._replicaId, opId.counter);\n   323→      return {\n   324→        type: \"delete\",\n   325→        id: opId,\n   326→        ranges: [],\n   327→        version: cloneVersionVector(this._version),\n   328→      };\n   329→    }\n   330→\n   331→    return this.deleteInternal(clampedStart, clampedEnd);\n   332→  }\n   333→\n   334→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   335→  // Transactions\n   336→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   337→\n   338→  /**\n   339→   * Flush the current implicit (time-based) transaction onto the undo stack.\n   340→   * Called when starting an explicit transaction, on undo/redo, or when the\n   341→   * time window expires.\n   342→   */\n   343→  private flushImplicitTransaction(): void {\n   344→    if (this.implicitTransaction !== null && this.implicitTransaction.operationIds.length > 0) {\n   345→      this.transactionHistory.push(this.implicitTransaction);\n   346→      this.undoStack.push({\n   347→        transactionId: this.implicitTransaction.id,\n   348→        operationIds: [...this.implicitTransaction.operationIds],\n   349→        undoCounts: [],\n   350→      });\n   351→      this.redoStack = [];\n   352→    }\n   353→    this.implicitTransaction = null;\n   354→    this.lastEditType = null;\n   355→  }\n   356→\n   357→  /**\n   358→   * Record an operation into the implicit (time-based) transaction grouping.\n   359→   * Consecutive same-type edits within `groupDelay` ms share one transaction.\n   360→   */\n   361→  private recordImplicitOp(opId: OperationId, editType: \"insert\" | \"delete\"): void {\n   362→    const now = this._now();\n   363→\n   364→    if (this.implicitTransaction !== null) {\n   365→      const elapsed = now - this.implicitTransaction.timestamp;\n   366→      if (editType === this.lastEditType && this.groupDelay > 0 && elapsed <= this.groupDelay) {\n   367→        // Append to existing implicit transaction\n   368→        this.implicitTransaction.operationIds.push(opId);\n   369→        this.implicitTransaction.timestamp = now;\n   370→        return;\n   371→      }\n   372→      // Different type or time window expired — flush the old one\n   373→      this.flushImplicitTransaction();\n   374→    }\n   375→\n   376→    // Start a new implicit transaction\n   377→    const txnId = transactionId(this.nextTransactionId++);\n   378→    this.implicitTransaction = { id: txnId, operationIds: [opId], timestamp: now };\n   379→    this.lastEditType = editType;\n   380→    // Clear redo stack on new edit\n   381→    this.redoStack = [];\n   382→  }\n   383→\n   384→  /**\n   385→   * Start a new explicit transaction. All operations until endTransaction()\n   386→   * are grouped into one undo unit, regardless of time or edit type.\n   387→   */\n   388→  startTransaction(): TransactionId {\n   389→    // Flush any pending implicit transaction first\n   390→    this.flushImplicitTransaction();\n   391→    const id = transactionId(this.nextTransactionId++);\n   392→    this.activeTransaction = { id, operationIds: [], timestamp: this._now() };\n   393→    return id;\n   394→  }\n   395→\n   396→  /**\n   397→   * End the current explicit transaction.\n   398→   */\n   399→  endTransaction(): TransactionId {\n   400→    if (this.activeTransaction === null) {\n   401→      return transactionId(-1);\n   402→    }\n   403→    const txn = this.activeTransaction;\n   404→    this.activeTransaction = null;\n   405→    if (txn.operationIds.length > 0) {\n   406→      this.transactionHistory.push(txn);\n   407→      this.undoStack.push({\n   408→        transactionId: txn.id,\n   409→        operationIds: [...txn.operationIds],\n   410→        undoCounts: [],\n   411→      });\n   412→      // Clear redo stack on new edit\n   413→      this.redoStack = [];\n   414→    }\n   415→    return txn.id;\n   416→  }\n   417→\n   418→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   419→  // Undo / Redo\n   420→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   421→\n   422→  /**\n   423→   * Undo the most recent transaction.\n   424→   * Returns an UndoOperation for collaboration, or null if nothing to undo.\n   425→   */\n   426→  undo(): Operation | null {\n   427→    // Flush any pending implicit transaction so it becomes undoable\n   428→    this.flushImplicitTransaction();\n   429→    const entry = this.undoStack.pop();\n   430→    if (entry === undefined) {\n   431→      return null;\n   432→    }\n   433→\n   434→    const opId = this.clock.tick();\n   435→    observeVersion(this._version, this._replicaId, opId.counter);\n   436→\n   437→    const undoCounts: Array<{ operationId: OperationId; oldCount: number; newCount: number }> = [];\n   438→\n   439→    // Increment undo count for each operation in the transaction\n   440→    for (const targetOpId of entry.operationIds) {\n   441→      const oldCount = this.undoMap.getCount(targetOpId);\n   442→      const newCount = this.undoMap.increment(targetOpId);\n   443→      undoCounts.push({ operationId: targetOpId, oldCount, newCount });\n   444→    }\n   445→\n   446→    // Recompute visibility for all affected fragments\n   447→    this.recomputeVisibility();\n   448→\n   449→    // Push to redo stack\n   450→    this.redoStack.push({\n   451→      transactionId: entry.transactionId,\n   452→      operationIds: entry.operationIds,\n   453→      undoCounts,\n   454→    });\n   455→\n   456→    return {\n   457→      type: \"undo\",\n   458→      id: opId,\n   459→      transactionId: entry.transactionId,\n   460→      counts: undoCounts.map((e) => ({ operationId: e.operationId, count: e.newCount })),\n   461→      version: cloneVersionVector(this._version),\n   462→    };\n   463→  }\n   464→\n   465→  /**\n   466→   * Redo the most recently undone transaction.\n   467→   * Returns an UndoOperation for collaboration, or null if nothing to redo.\n   468→   */\n   469→  redo(): Operation | null {\n   470→    // Flush any pending implicit transaction first\n   471→    this.flushImplicitTransaction();\n   472→    const entry = this.redoStack.pop();\n   473→    if (entry === undefined) {\n   474→      return null;\n   475→    }\n   476→\n   477→    const opId = this.clock.tick();\n   478→    observeVersion(this._version, this._replicaId, opId.counter);\n   479→\n   480→    const undoCounts: Array<{ operationId: OperationId; oldCount: number; newCount: number }> = [];\n   481→\n   482→    // Increment undo count again (even count = done)\n   483→    for (const targetOpId of entry.operationIds) {\n   484→      const oldCount = this.undoMap.getCount(targetOpId);\n   485→      const newCount = this.undoMap.increment(targetOpId);\n   486→      undoCounts.push({ operationId: targetOpId, oldCount, newCount });\n   487→    }\n   488→\n   489→    // Recompute visibility\n   490→    this.recomputeVisibility();\n   491→\n   492→    // Push back to undo stack\n   493→    this.undoStack.push({\n   494→      transactionId: entry.transactionId,\n   495→      operationIds: entry.operationIds,\n   496→      undoCounts,\n   497→    });\n   498→\n   499→    return {\n   500→      type: \"undo\",\n   501→      id: opId,\n   502→      transactionId: entry.transactionId,\n   503→      counts: undoCounts.map((e) => ({ operationId: e.operationId, count: e.newCount })),\n   504→      version: cloneVersionVector(this._version),\n   505→    };\n   506→  }\n   507→\n   508→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   509→  // Snapshots\n   510→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   511→\n   512→  /**\n   513→   * Create an immutable O(1) snapshot of the current buffer state.\n   514→   *\n   515→   * The snapshot captures:\n   516→   * - Root NodeId of the fragment SumTree (O(1))\n   517→   * - Cloned VersionVector (O(replicas))\n   518→   * - Epoch for reclamation tracking\n   519→   *\n   520→   * Call snapshot.release() when done to enable memory reclamation.\n   521→   */\n   522→  snapshot(options?: SnapshotOptions): TextBufferSnapshot {\n   523→    // Advance epoch and get the new one for this snapshot\n   524→    const arena = this.fragments.getArena();\n   525→    const snapshotEpoch = arena.advanceEpoch();\n   526→\n   527→    // Retain the epoch (increment ref count)\n   528→    arena.retainEpoch(snapshotEpoch);\n   529→    this._liveSnapshots++;\n   530→\n   531→    // Create a shallow clone of the tree (O(1))\n   532→    const treeSnapshot = this.fragments.snapshotClone();\n   533→\n   534→    // Create the snapshot with release callback\n   535→    return new TextBufferSnapshot(treeSnapshot, cloneVersionVector(this._version), snapshotEpoch, {\n   536→      ...options,\n   537→      onRelease: (epoch: Epoch, wasAutoRelease: boolean) => {\n   538→        this.onSnapshotReleased(epoch, wasAutoRelease);\n   539→        // Call user's onRelease if provided\n   540→        if (options?.onRelease) {\n   541→          options.onRelease(epoch, wasAutoRelease);\n   542→        }\n   543→      },\n   544→    });\n   545→  }\n   546→\n   547→  /**\n   548→   * Called when a snapshot is released. Updates epoch tracking.\n   549→   */\n   550→  private onSnapshotReleased(epoch: Epoch, _wasAutoRelease: boolean): void {\n   551→    const arena = this.fragments.getArena();\n   552→    arena.releaseEpoch(epoch);\n   553→    this._liveSnapshots = Math.max(0, this._liveSnapshots - 1);\n   554→  }\n   555→\n   556→  /** Number of live (unreleased) snapshots. */\n   557→  get liveSnapshots(): number {\n   558→    return this._liveSnapshots;\n   559→  }\n   560→\n   561→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   562→  // Arena utilization and garbage collection\n   563→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   564→\n   565→  /**\n   566→   * Get utilization statistics for the underlying arena.\n   567→   */\n   568→  arenaUtilization(): import(\"../arena/index.js\").ArenaUtilization {\n   569→    return this.fragments.getArena().utilization();\n   570→  }\n   571→\n   572→  /**\n   573→   * Perform garbage collection on the arena.\n   574→   * Frees nodes that are no longer reachable from any live snapshot or the current tree.\n   575→   * Returns the number of nodes freed.\n   576→   */\n   577→  collectGarbage(): number {\n   578→    const arena = this.fragments.getArena();\n   579→    return arena.collectGarbage([this.fragments.root]);\n   580→  }\n   581→\n   582→  /**\n   583→   * Get the current root NodeId of the fragment tree.\n   584→   * Useful for debugging and testing structural sharing.\n   585→   */\n   586→  get fragmentsRoot(): NodeId {\n   587→    return this.fragments.root;\n   588→  }\n   589→\n   590→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   591→  // Collaboration\n   592→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   593→\n   594→  /**\n   595→   * Apply a remote operation to this buffer.\n   596→   */\n   597→  applyRemote(operation: Operation): void {\n   598→    // Idempotency check: skip operations that have already been applied.\n   599→    const opKey = `${operation.id.replicaId}:${operation.id.counter}`;\n   600→    if (this.appliedOps.has(opKey)) {\n   601→      return; // Already applied\n   602→    }\n   603→\n   604→    // Check causal readiness: all operations that the sender had seen\n   605→    // when generating this operation must already be applied here.\n   606→    if (!this.isCausallyReady(operation)) {\n   607→      this.pendingOps.push(operation);\n   608→      return;\n   609→    }\n   610→\n   611→    this.applyRemoteInternal(operation);\n   612→    this.retryPendingOps();\n   613→  }\n   614→\n   615→  /**\n   616→   * Check if an operation's causal dependencies are satisfied.\n   617→   * For inserts: the referenced after/before fragments must exist.\n   618→   * For deletes: the referenced fragments must exist.\n   619→   * For undos: always ready (undo counts are merged via max-wins).\n   620→   */\n   621→  private isCausallyReady(operation: Operation): boolean {\n   622→    switch (operation.type) {\n   623→      case \"insert\":\n   624→        return this.isInsertReady(operation);\n   625→      case \"delete\":\n   626→        return this.isDeleteReady(operation);\n   627→      case \"undo\":\n   628→        return true;\n   629→    }\n   630→  }\n   631→\n   632→  /** Check if an insert operation's after/before fragments exist. */\n   633→  private isInsertReady(op: InsertOperation): boolean {\n   634→    if (!operationIdsEqual(op.after.insertionId, MIN_OPERATION_ID)) {\n   635→      if (!this.hasFragment(op.after.insertionId)) {\n   636→        return false;\n   637→      }\n   638→    }\n   639→    if (!operationIdsEqual(op.before.insertionId, MAX_OPERATION_ID)) {\n   640→      if (!this.hasFragment(op.before.insertionId)) {\n   641→        return false;\n   642→      }\n   643→    }\n   644→    return true;\n   645→  }\n   646→\n   647→  /** Check if a delete operation's target fragments exist. */\n   648→  private isDeleteReady(op: DeleteOperation): boolean {\n   649→    for (const range of op.ranges) {\n   650→      if (!this.hasFragment(range.insertionId)) {\n   651→        return false;\n   652→      }\n   653→    }\n   654→    return true;\n   655→  }\n   656→\n   657→  /** Check if any fragment with the given insertionId exists. */\n   658→  private hasFragment(insertionId: OperationId): boolean {\n   659→    for (const frag of this.fragmentsArray()) {\n   660→      if (operationIdsEqual(frag.insertionId, insertionId)) {\n   661→        return true;\n   662→      }\n   663→    }\n   664→    return false;\n   665→  }\n   666→\n   667→  /**\n   668→   * Apply a remote operation that has been confirmed as causally ready.\n   669→   */\n   670→  private applyRemoteInternal(operation: Operation): void {\n   671→    const opKey = `${operation.id.replicaId}:${operation.id.counter}`;\n   672→    this.appliedOps.add(opKey);\n   673→\n   674→    switch (operation.type) {\n   675→      case \"insert\":\n   676→        this.applyRemoteInsertDirect(operation);\n   677→        break;\n   678→      case \"delete\":\n   679→        this.applyRemoteDelete(operation);\n   680→        break;\n   681→      case \"undo\":\n   682→        this.applyRemoteUndo(operation);\n   683→        break;\n   684→    }\n   685→  }\n   686→\n   687→  /**\n   688→   * Retry pending operations that may now have their causal dependencies satisfied.\n   689→   */\n   690→  private retryPendingOps(): void {\n   691→    let madeProgress = true;\n   692→    while (madeProgress) {\n   693→      madeProgress = false;\n   694→      const stillPending: Operation[] = [];\n   695→\n   696→      for (const pendingOp of this.pendingOps) {\n   697→        const opKey = `${pendingOp.id.replicaId}:${pendingOp.id.counter}`;\n   698→        if (this.appliedOps.has(opKey)) {\n   699→          continue; // Already applied (idempotency)\n   700→        }\n   701→        if (this.isCausallyReady(pendingOp)) {\n   702→          this.applyRemoteInternal(pendingOp);\n   703→          madeProgress = true;\n   704→        } else {\n   705→          stillPending.push(pendingOp);\n   706→        }\n   707→      }\n   708→\n   709→      this.pendingOps = stillPending;\n   710→    }\n   711→  }\n   712→\n   713→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   714→  // Internal: insert\n   715→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   716→\n   717→  private insertInternal(offset: number, text: string): InsertOperation {\n   718→    const opId = this.clock.tick();\n   719→    observeVersion(this._version, this._replicaId, opId.counter);\n   720→\n   721→    // Record in active (explicit) transaction or implicit (time-based) group\n   722→    if (this.activeTransaction !== null) {\n   723→      this.activeTransaction.operationIds.push(opId);\n   724→    } else {\n   725→      this.recordImplicitOp(opId, \"insert\");\n   726→    }\n   727→\n   728→    const frags = this.fragmentsArray();\n   729→\n   730→    // Find the position to insert: seek to the visible offset\n   731→    const { leftLocator, rightLocator, insertLocator, afterRef, beforeRef } =\n   732→      this.findInsertPosition(frags, offset);\n   733→\n   734→    // Use explicit insertLocator if provided (for split cases), otherwise compute via locatorBetween\n   735→    const locator = insertLocator ?? locatorBetween(leftLocator, rightLocator);\n   736→\n   737→    // Create the new fragment\n   738→    const newFrag = createFragment(opId, 0, locator, text, true);\n   739→\n   740→    // Insert and sort using the same approach as remote inserts for consistency.\n   741→    this.insertFragmentByLocator(frags, newFrag);\n   742→    sortFragments(frags);\n   743→    this.fragments = SumTree.fromItems(frags, fragmentSummaryOps);\n   744→\n   745→    return {\n   746→      type: \"insert\",\n   747→      id: opId,\n   748→      text,\n   749→      after: afterRef,\n   750→      before: beforeRef,\n   751→      version: cloneVersionVector(this._version),\n   752→      locator,\n   753→    };\n   754→  }\n   755→\n   756→  private findInsertPosition(\n   757→    frags: Fragment[],\n   758→    offset: number,\n   759→  ): {\n   760→    leftLocator: Locator;\n   761→    rightLocator: Locator;\n   762→    /** Explicit Locator for the new insert (computed for split cases to avoid collisions) */\n   763→    insertLocator?: Locator;\n   764→    insertIndex: number;\n   765→    afterRef: { insertionId: OperationId; offset: number };\n   766→    beforeRef: { insertionId: OperationId; offset: number };\n   767→  } {\n   768→    if (frags.length === 0) {\n   769→      return {\n   770→        leftLocator: MIN_LOCATOR,\n   771→        rightLocator: MAX_LOCATOR,\n   772→        insertIndex: 0,\n   773→        afterRef: { insertionId: MIN_OPERATION_ID, offset: 0 },\n   774→        beforeRef: { insertionId: MAX_OPERATION_ID, offset: 0 },\n   775→      };\n   776→    }\n   777→\n   778→    let visibleOffset = 0;\n   779→\n   780→    for (let i = 0; i < frags.length; i++) {\n   781→      const frag = frags[i];\n   782→      if (frag === undefined) continue;\n   783→\n   784→      if (frag.visible) {\n   785→        if (visibleOffset + frag.length > offset) {\n   786→          const localOffset = offset - visibleOffset;\n   787→\n   788→          // If localOffset === 0, insert BEFORE this fragment (at boundary)\n   789→          // Don't split — that would create a zero-length fragment and use 2*0-1 = -1\n   790→          if (localOffset === 0) {\n   791→            const leftLocator = i > 0 ? (frags[i - 1]?.locator ?? MIN_LOCATOR) : MIN_LOCATOR;\n   792→            const rightLocator = frag.locator;\n   793→\n   794→            return {\n   795→              leftLocator,\n   796→              rightLocator,\n   797→              insertIndex: i,\n   798→              afterRef:\n   799→                i > 0 && frags[i - 1] !== undefined\n   800→                  ? {\n   801→                      insertionId: frags[i - 1]!.insertionId,\n   802→                      offset: frags[i - 1]!.insertionOffset + frags[i - 1]!.length,\n   803→                    }\n   804→                  : { insertionId: MIN_OPERATION_ID, offset: 0 },\n   805→              beforeRef: {\n   806→                insertionId: frag.insertionId,\n   807→                offset: frag.insertionOffset,\n   808→              },\n   809→            };\n   810→          }\n   811→\n   812→          // The insert point is strictly inside this fragment — split it\n   813→          const [left, right] = splitFragment(frag, localOffset);\n   814→\n   815→          // Replace the original fragment with the split pair\n   816→          frags.splice(i, 1, left, right);\n   817→\n   818→          // Compute explicit Locator using the 2*k-1 scheme to avoid collisions\n   819→          // with the 2*k scheme used for split fragments\n   820→          const k = right.insertionOffset;\n   821→          const insertLocator: Locator = {\n   822→            levels: [...frag.baseLocator.levels, 2 * k - 1],\n   823→          };\n   824→\n   825→          return {\n   826→            leftLocator: left.locator,\n   827→            rightLocator: right.locator,\n   828→            insertLocator,\n   829→            insertIndex: i + 1,\n   830→            afterRef: {\n   831→              insertionId: left.insertionId,\n   832→              offset: left.insertionOffset + left.length,\n   833→            },\n   834→            beforeRef: {\n   835→              insertionId: right.insertionId,\n   836→              offset: right.insertionOffset,\n   837→            },\n   838→          };\n   839→        }\n   840→        visibleOffset += frag.length;\n   841→\n   842→        if (visibleOffset === offset) {\n   843→          // Insert right after this fragment\n   844→          const leftLocator = frag.locator;\n   845→          const rightLocator =\n   846→            i + 1 < frags.length ? (frags[i + 1]?.locator ?? MAX_LOCATOR) : MAX_LOCATOR;\n   847→\n   848→          return {\n   849→            leftLocator,\n   850→            rightLocator,\n   851→            insertIndex: i + 1,\n   852→            afterRef: {\n   853→              insertionId: frag.insertionId,\n   854→              offset: frag.insertionOffset + frag.length,\n   855→            },\n   856→            beforeRef: (() => {\n   857→              const nextFrag = frags[i + 1];\n   858→              if (nextFrag !== undefined) {\n   859→                return {\n   860→                  insertionId: nextFrag.insertionId,\n   861→                  offset: nextFrag.insertionOffset,\n   862→                };\n   863→              }\n   864→              return { insertionId: MAX_OPERATION_ID, offset: 0 };\n   865→            })(),\n   866→          };\n   867→        }\n   868→      }\n   869→    }\n   870→\n   871→    // Insert at the end\n   872→    const lastFrag = frags[frags.length - 1];\n   873→    return {\n   874→      leftLocator: lastFrag !== undefined ? lastFrag.locator : MIN_LOCATOR,\n   875→      rightLocator: MAX_LOCATOR,\n   876→      insertIndex: frags.length,\n   877→      afterRef:\n   878→        lastFrag !== undefined\n   879→          ? {\n   880→              insertionId: lastFrag.insertionId,\n   881→              offset: lastFrag.insertionOffset + lastFrag.length,\n   882→            }\n   883→          : { insertionId: MIN_OPERATION_ID, offset: 0 },\n   884→      beforeRef: { insertionId: MAX_OPERATION_ID, offset: 0 },\n   885→    };\n   886→  }\n   887→\n   888→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   889→  // Internal: delete\n   890→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   891→\n   892→  private deleteInternal(start: number, end: number): DeleteOperation {\n   893→    const opId = this.clock.tick();\n   894→    observeVersion(this._version, this._replicaId, opId.counter);\n   895→\n   896→    // Record in active (explicit) transaction or implicit (time-based) group\n   897→    if (this.activeTransaction !== null) {\n   898→      this.activeTransaction.operationIds.push(opId);\n   899→    } else {\n   900→      this.recordImplicitOp(opId, \"delete\");\n   901→    }\n   902→\n   903→    const frags = this.fragmentsArray();\n   904→    const newFrags: Fragment[] = [];\n   905→    const ranges: Array<{ insertionId: OperationId; offset: number; length: number }> = [];\n   906→\n   907→    let visibleOffset = 0;\n   908→\n   909→    for (let i = 0; i < frags.length; i++) {\n   910→      const frag = frags[i];\n   911→      if (frag === undefined) continue;\n   912→\n   913→      if (!frag.visible) {\n   914→        newFrags.push(frag);\n   915→        continue;\n   916→      }\n   917→\n   918→      const fragStart = visibleOffset;\n   919→      const fragEnd = visibleOffset + frag.length;\n   920→\n   921→      if (fragEnd <= start || fragStart >= end) {\n   922→        // Fragment is entirely outside the delete range\n   923→        newFrags.push(frag);\n   924→      } else if (fragStart >= start && fragEnd <= end) {\n   925→        // Fragment is entirely within the delete range\n   926→        newFrags.push(deleteFragment(frag, opId));\n   927→        ranges.push({\n   928→          insertionId: frag.insertionId,\n   929→          offset: frag.insertionOffset,\n   930→          length: frag.length,\n   931→        });\n   932→      } else if (fragStart < start && fragEnd > end) {\n   933→        // Delete range is entirely within this fragment — split into 3 parts\n   934→        const deleteStart = start - fragStart;\n   935→        const deleteEnd = end - fragStart;\n   936→\n   937→        const [beforePart, rest] = splitFragment(frag, deleteStart);\n   938→        const [deletedPart, afterPart] = splitFragment(rest, deleteEnd - deleteStart);\n   939→\n   940→        newFrags.push(beforePart);\n   941→        newFrags.push(deleteFragment(deletedPart, opId));\n   942→        newFrags.push(afterPart);\n   943→\n   944→        ranges.push({\n   945→          insertionId: deletedPart.insertionId,\n   946→          offset: deletedPart.insertionOffset,\n   947→          length: deletedPart.length,\n   948→        });\n   949→      } else if (fragStart < start) {\n   950→        // Delete range overlaps the end of this fragment\n   951→        const splitPoint = start - fragStart;\n   952→        const [keepPart, deletedPart] = splitFragment(frag, splitPoint);\n   953→\n   954→        newFrags.push(keepPart);\n   955→        newFrags.push(deleteFragment(deletedPart, opId));\n   956→\n   957→        ranges.push({\n   958→          insertionId: deletedPart.insertionId,\n   959→          offset: deletedPart.insertionOffset,\n   960→          length: deletedPart.length,\n   961→        });\n   962→      } else {\n   963→        // Delete range overlaps the start of this fragment (fragEnd > end)\n   964→        const splitPoint = end - fragStart;\n   965→        const [deletedPart, keepPart] = splitFragment(frag, splitPoint);\n   966→\n   967→        newFrags.push(deleteFragment(deletedPart, opId));\n   968→        newFrags.push(keepPart);\n   969→\n   970→        ranges.push({\n   971→          insertionId: deletedPart.insertionId,\n   972→          offset: deletedPart.insertionOffset,\n   973→          length: deletedPart.length,\n   974→        });\n   975→      }\n   976→\n   977→      visibleOffset = fragEnd;\n   978→    }\n   979→\n   980→    this.fragments = SumTree.fromItems(newFrags, fragmentSummaryOps);\n   981→\n   982→    return {\n   983→      type: \"delete\",\n   984→      id: opId,\n   985→      ranges,\n   986→      version: cloneVersionVector(this._version),\n   987→    };\n   988→  }\n   989→\n   990→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   991→  // Internal: recompute visibility after undo/redo\n   992→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   993→\n   994→  private recomputeVisibility(): void {\n   995→    const frags = this.fragmentsArray();\n   996→    let changed = false;\n   997→    const newFrags: Fragment[] = [];\n   998→\n   999→    for (const frag of frags) {\n  1000→      const shouldBeVisible = this.undoMap.isVisible(frag.insertionId, frag.deletions);\n  1001→      if (shouldBeVisible !== frag.visible) {\n  1002→        newFrags.push(withVisibility(frag, shouldBeVisible));\n  1003→        changed = true;\n  1004→      } else {\n  1005→        newFrags.push(frag);\n  1006→      }\n  1007→    }\n  1008→\n  1009→    if (changed) {\n  1010→      this.fragments = SumTree.fromItems(newFrags, fragmentSummaryOps);\n  1011→    }\n  1012→  }\n  1013→\n  1014→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  1015→  // Internal: apply remote operations\n  1016→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  1017→\n  1018→  /**\n  1019→   * Apply a remote insert operation. Causal ordering is guaranteed by the\n  1020→   * caller (applyRemote), so all referenced fragments should exist.\n  1021→   *\n  1022→   * Key insight: the operation's Locator determines its canonical position.\n  1023→   * We binary search the ENTIRE array by Locator — the after/before refs are\n  1024→   * only for causal ordering (ensuring dependencies exist), not positioning.\n  1025→   */\n  1026→  private applyRemoteInsertDirect(op: InsertOperation): void {\n  1027→    // Update clock\n  1028→    this.clock.observe(op.id.counter);\n  1029→    observeVersion(this._version, op.id.replicaId, op.id.counter);\n  1030→    mergeVersionVectors(this._version, op.version);\n  1031→\n  1032→    const frags = this.fragmentsArray();\n  1033→\n  1034→    // findRefIndex calls may split fragments, which is necessary for causal\n  1035→    // correctness, but we don't use the returned indices for positioning.\n  1036→    if (!operationIdsEqual(op.after.insertionId, MIN_OPERATION_ID)) {\n  1037→      this.findRefIndex(frags, op.after, \"after\");\n  1038→    }\n  1039→    if (!operationIdsEqual(op.before.insertionId, MAX_OPERATION_ID)) {\n  1040→      this.findRefIndex(frags, op.before, \"before\");\n  1041→    }\n  1042→\n  1043→    // After splits, the array may not be in locator order. Re-sort it.\n  1044→    sortFragments(frags);\n  1045→\n  1046→    // Create the new fragment with its original locator.\n  1047→    // The sort function handles interleaving with children based on operation ID.\n  1048→    const newFrag = createFragment(op.id, 0, op.locator, op.text, true);\n  1049→\n  1050→    // Insert the fragment at its locator-sorted position\n  1051→    this.insertFragmentByLocator(frags, newFrag);\n  1052→\n  1053→    // Re-sort after insertion to ensure correct interleaving\n  1054→    sortFragments(frags);\n  1055→\n  1056→    this.fragments = SumTree.fromItems(frags, fragmentSummaryOps);\n  1057→  }\n  1058→\n  1059→  /**\n  1060→   * Insert a fragment into the array at its locator-sorted position.\n  1061→   * Modifies the array in place.\n  1062→   */\n  1063→  private insertFragmentByLocator(frags: Fragment[], newFrag: Fragment): void {\n  1064→    let insertIndex = 0;\n  1065→    for (let i = 0; i < frags.length; i++) {\n  1066→      const frag = frags[i];\n  1067→      if (frag === undefined) continue;\n  1068→\n  1069→      const cmp = compareLocators(newFrag.locator, frag.locator);\n  1070→      if (cmp < 0) {\n  1071→        break;\n  1072→      }\n  1073→      if (cmp === 0) {\n  1074→        // Same locator — tie-break by (replicaId, counter, insertionOffset)\n  1075→        const idCmp = compareOperationIds(newFrag.insertionId, frag.insertionId);\n  1076→        if (idCmp < 0) {\n  1077→          break;\n  1078→        }\n  1079→        if (idCmp === 0) {\n  1080→          // Same operation — compare insertionOffset\n  1081→          if (newFrag.insertionOffset < frag.insertionOffset) {\n  1082→            break;\n  1083→          }\n  1084→        }\n  1085→      }\n  1086→      insertIndex = i + 1;\n  1087→    }\n  1088→    frags.splice(insertIndex, 0, newFrag);\n  1089→  }\n  1090→\n  1091→  /**\n  1092→   * Find the index of a fragment referenced by an after/before ref.\n  1093→   * Splits the fragment if the reference point falls inside it.\n  1094→   * Returns the index, or null if the referenced fragment doesn't exist.\n  1095→   *\n  1096→   * For \"after\": returns the index of the fragment AFTER which to insert.\n  1097→   * For \"before\": returns the index of the fragment BEFORE which to insert.\n  1098→   */\n  1099→  private findRefIndex(\n  1100→    frags: Fragment[],\n  1101→    ref: { insertionId: OperationId; offset: number },\n  1102→    type: \"after\" | \"before\",\n  1103→  ): number | null {\n  1104→    // Collect indices of all fragments with the matching insertionId\n  1105→    const matchingIndices: number[] = [];\n  1106→    for (let i = 0; i < frags.length; i++) {\n  1107→      const frag = frags[i];\n  1108→      if (frag === undefined) continue;\n  1109→      if (operationIdsEqual(frag.insertionId, ref.insertionId)) {\n  1110→        matchingIndices.push(i);\n  1111→      }\n  1112→    }\n  1113→\n  1114→    if (matchingIndices.length === 0) {\n  1115→      return null; // Insertion not found — dependency not yet applied\n  1116→    }\n  1117→\n  1118→    // Check for exact matches and splits\n  1119→    for (const i of matchingIndices) {\n  1120→      const frag = frags[i];\n  1121→      if (frag === undefined) continue;\n  1122→      const fragEnd = frag.insertionOffset + frag.length;\n  1123→\n  1124→      // Case 1: Reference falls strictly inside this fragment — split it\n  1125→      if (ref.offset > frag.insertionOffset && ref.offset < fragEnd) {\n  1126→        const splitPoint = ref.offset - frag.insertionOffset;\n  1127→        const [leftPart, rightPart] = splitFragment(frag, splitPoint);\n  1128→        frags.splice(i, 1, leftPart, rightPart);\n  1129→        return type === \"after\" ? i : i + 1;\n  1130→      }\n  1131→\n  1132→      if (type === \"after\") {\n  1133→        // Case 2: Fragment ends exactly at the reference offset\n  1134→        if (fragEnd === ref.offset) {\n  1135→          return i;\n  1136→        }\n  1137→      }\n  1138→\n  1139→      if (type === \"before\") {\n  1140→        // Case 3: Fragment starts exactly at the reference offset\n  1141→        // Prefer non-zero-length fragments to avoid matching zero-length splits\n  1142→        // that are meant for \"after\" references.\n  1143→        if (frag.insertionOffset === ref.offset && frag.length > 0) {\n  1144→          return i;\n  1145→        }\n  1146→      }\n  1147→    }\n  1148→\n  1149→    // Edge cases: the reference points to a boundary that requires\n  1150→    // a zero-length split to exist (the sender created one during local editing).\n  1151→    if (type === \"after\") {\n  1152→      // \"after offset X\" where X equals the insertionOffset of the first\n  1153→      // matching fragment. This means the sender had a zero-length left split.\n  1154→      const firstIdx = matchingIndices[0];\n  1155→      if (firstIdx === undefined) return null;\n  1156→      const firstFrag = frags[firstIdx];\n  1157→      if (\n  1158→        firstFrag !== undefined &&\n  1159→        firstFrag.insertionOffset === ref.offset &&\n  1160→        firstFrag.length > 0\n  1161→      ) {\n  1162→        // Create zero-length left split to match sender state\n  1163→        const [leftPart, rightPart] = splitFragment(firstFrag, 0);\n  1164→        frags.splice(firstIdx, 1, leftPart, rightPart);\n  1165→        return firstIdx; // Return the zero-length fragment's index\n  1166→      }\n  1167→    }\n  1168→\n  1169→    if (type === \"before\") {\n  1170→      // \"before offset X\" where X equals the end of the last matching fragment.\n  1171→      // This means the sender had a zero-length right split.\n  1172→      const lastIdx = matchingIndices[matchingIndices.length - 1];\n  1173→      if (lastIdx === undefined) return null;\n  1174→      const lastFrag = frags[lastIdx];\n  1175→      if (lastFrag !== undefined) {\n  1176→        const lastEnd = lastFrag.insertionOffset + lastFrag.length;\n  1177→        if (lastEnd === ref.offset && lastFrag.length > 0) {\n  1178→          // Create zero-length right split to match sender state\n  1179→          const [leftPart, rightPart] = splitFragment(lastFrag, lastFrag.length);\n  1180→          frags.splice(lastIdx, 1, leftPart, rightPart);\n  1181→          return lastIdx + 1; // Return the zero-length fragment's index\n  1182→        }\n  1183→      }\n  1184→    }\n  1185→\n  1186→    return null; // Reference not found\n  1187→  }\n  1188→\n  1189→  private applyRemoteDelete(op: DeleteOperation): void {\n  1190→    this.clock.observe(op.id.counter);\n  1191→    observeVersion(this._version, op.id.replicaId, op.id.counter);\n  1192→    mergeVersionVectors(this._version, op.version);\n  1193→\n  1194→    // Use a work list approach: when a fragment is split, the resulting parts\n  1195→    // may still overlap with other delete ranges and need re-processing.\n  1196→    const workList = [...this.fragmentsArray()];\n  1197→    const resultFrags: Fragment[] = [];\n  1198→\n  1199→    while (workList.length > 0) {\n  1200→      const frag = workList.shift();\n  1201→      if (frag === undefined) break; // Should never happen, but satisfies lint\n  1202→      let wasProcessed = false;\n  1203→\n  1204→      for (const range of op.ranges) {\n  1205→        if (!operationIdsEqual(frag.insertionId, range.insertionId)) {\n  1206→          continue;\n  1207→        }\n  1208→\n  1209→        const fragStart = frag.insertionOffset;\n  1210→        const fragEnd = frag.insertionOffset + frag.length;\n  1211→        const rangeStart = range.offset;\n  1212→        const rangeEnd = range.offset + range.length;\n  1213→\n  1214→        // Check for any overlap between the fragment and the delete range\n  1215→        if (fragEnd <= rangeStart || fragStart >= rangeEnd) {\n  1216→          // No overlap with this range\n  1217→          continue;\n  1218→        }\n  1219→\n  1220→        if (fragStart >= rangeStart && fragEnd <= rangeEnd) {\n  1221→          // Fragment is entirely within the delete range - done with this fragment\n  1222→          resultFrags.push(deleteFragment(frag, op.id));\n  1223→          wasProcessed = true;\n  1224→          break;\n  1225→        }\n  1226→\n  1227→        if (fragStart < rangeStart && fragEnd > rangeEnd) {\n  1228→          // Delete range is entirely within this fragment — split into 3 parts\n  1229→          // The \"after\" part might still overlap with other ranges, so re-check it.\n  1230→          const deleteLocalStart = rangeStart - fragStart;\n  1231→          const deleteLocalEnd = rangeEnd - fragStart;\n  1232→\n  1233→          const [beforePart, rest] = splitFragment(frag, deleteLocalStart);\n  1234→          const [deletedPart, afterPart] = splitFragment(rest, deleteLocalEnd - deleteLocalStart);\n  1235→\n  1236→          resultFrags.push(beforePart);\n  1237→          resultFrags.push(deleteFragment(deletedPart, op.id));\n  1238→          workList.unshift(afterPart); // Re-check against remaining ranges\n  1239→          wasProcessed = true;\n  1240→          break;\n  1241→        }\n  1242→\n  1243→        if (fragStart < rangeStart) {\n  1244→          // Delete range overlaps the end of this fragment\n  1245→          const splitPoint = rangeStart - fragStart;\n  1246→          const [keepPart, deletedPart] = splitFragment(frag, splitPoint);\n  1247→\n  1248→          resultFrags.push(keepPart);\n  1249→          resultFrags.push(deleteFragment(deletedPart, op.id));\n  1250→          wasProcessed = true;\n  1251→          break;\n  1252→        }\n  1253→\n  1254→        // Delete range overlaps the start of this fragment (fragEnd > rangeEnd)\n  1255→        // The \"keep\" part might still overlap with other ranges, so re-check it.\n  1256→        const splitPoint = rangeEnd - fragStart;\n  1257→        const [deletedPart, keepPart] = splitFragment(frag, splitPoint);\n  1258→\n  1259→        resultFrags.push(deleteFragment(deletedPart, op.id));\n  1260→        workList.unshift(keepPart); // Re-check against remaining ranges\n  1261→        wasProcessed = true;\n  1262→        break;\n  1263→      }\n  1264→      if (!wasProcessed) {\n  1265→        resultFrags.push(frag);\n  1266→      }\n  1267→    }\n  1268→\n  1269→    // Sort by (locator, insertionId, insertionOffset) to maintain canonical order\n  1270→    // after splits. This matches the sorting in applyRemoteInsertDirect.\n  1271→    resultFrags.sort((a, b) => {\n  1272→      const locCmp = compareLocators(a.locator, b.locator);\n  1273→      if (locCmp !== 0) return locCmp;\n  1274→      const idCmp = compareOperationIds(a.insertionId, b.insertionId);\n  1275→      if (idCmp !== 0) return idCmp;\n  1276→      return a.insertionOffset - b.insertionOffset;\n  1277→    });\n  1278→\n  1279→    this.fragments = SumTree.fromItems(resultFrags, fragmentSummaryOps);\n  1280→  }\n  1281→\n  1282→  private applyRemoteUndo(op: UndoOperation): void {\n  1283→    this.clock.observe(op.id.counter);\n  1284→    observeVersion(this._version, op.id.replicaId, op.id.counter);\n  1285→    mergeVersionVectors(this._version, op.version);\n  1286→\n  1287→    // Apply undo counts with max-wins\n  1288→    this.undoMap.mergeFrom(op.counts);\n  1289→\n  1290→    // Recompute visibility\n  1291→    this.recomputeVisibility();\n  1292→  }\n  1293→\n  1294→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  1295→  // Helpers\n  1296→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  1297→\n  1298→  /** Get all fragments as an array. */\n  1299→  private fragmentsArray(): Fragment[] {\n  1300→    return this.fragments.toArray();\n  1301→  }\n  1302→\n  1303→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  1304→  // Serialization\n  1305→  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  1306→\n  1307→  /**\n  1308→   * Create a serializable snapshot of the complete CRDT state.\n  1309→   * This includes all data needed to fully reconstruct the buffer.\n  1310→   */\n  1311→  toSerializedSnapshot(): SerializedSnapshot {\n  1312→    // Serialize undo stack\n  1313→    const undoStack = this.undoStack.map((entry) => ({\n  1314→      txnId: entry.transactionId as number,\n  1315→      ops: entry.operationIds.map(serializeOperationId),\n  1316→      counts: entry.undoCounts.map((c) => ({\n  1317→        op: serializeOperationId(c.operationId),\n  1318→        old: c.oldCount,\n  1319→        new: c.newCount,\n  1320→      })),\n  1321→    }));\n  1322→\n  1323→    // Serialize redo stack\n  1324→    const redoStack = this.redoStack.map((entry) => ({\n  1325→      txnId: entry.transactionId as number,\n  1326→      ops: entry.operationIds.map(serializeOperationId),\n  1327→      counts: entry.undoCounts.map((c) => ({\n  1328→        op: serializeOperationId(c.operationId),\n  1329→        old: c.oldCount,\n  1330→        new: c.newCount,\n  1331→      })),\n  1332→    }));\n  1333→\n  1334→    // Serialize undo map\n  1335→    const undoMapEntries = this.undoMap.entries().map((e) => ({\n  1336→      op: serializeOperationId(e.operationId),\n  1337→      count: e.count,\n  1338→    }));\n  1339→\n  1340→    return {\n  1341→      version: SERIALIZATION_VERSION,\n  1342→      replicaId: this._replicaId as number,\n  1343→      clockCounter: this.clock.counter,\n  1344→      versionVector: serializeVersionVector(this._version),\n  1345→      fragments: this.fragmentsArray().map(serializeFragment),\n  1346→      undoMap: undoMapEntries,\n  1347→      undoStack,\n  1348→      redoStack,\n  1349→      appliedOps: [...this.appliedOps],\n  1350→      nextTransactionId: this.nextTransactionId,\n  1351→      groupDelay: this.groupDelay,\n  1352→    };\n  1353→  }\n  1354→\n  1355→  /**\n  1356→   * Serialize the buffer to a compact binary format.\n  1357→   * This is suitable for network transfer and persistent storage.\n  1358→   */\n  1359→  serialize(): Uint8Array {\n  1360→    return encodeBinary(this.toSerializedSnapshot());\n  1361→  }\n  1362→\n  1363→  /**\n  1364→   * Serialize the buffer to a JSON string.\n  1365→   * This is useful for debugging and human-readable storage.\n  1366→   */\n  1367→  serializeJSON(): string {\n  1368→    return encodeJSON(this.toSerializedSnapshot());\n  1369→  }\n  1370→\n  1371→  /**\n  1372→   * Restore a TextBuffer from a serialized binary snapshot.\n  1373→   */\n  1374→  static deserialize(data: Uint8Array): TextBuffer {\n  1375→    const snapshot = decodeBinary(data);\n  1376→    return TextBuffer.fromSerializedSnapshot(snapshot);\n  1377→  }\n  1378→\n  1379→  /**\n  1380→   * Restore a TextBuffer from a serialized JSON string.\n  1381→   */\n  1382→  static deserializeJSON(json: string): TextBuffer {\n  1383→    const snapshot = decodeJSON(json);\n  1384→    return TextBuffer.fromSerializedSnapshot(snapshot);\n  1385→  }\n  1386→\n  1387→  /**\n  1388→   * Restore a TextBuffer from a SerializedSnapshot.\n  1389→   */\n  1390→  static fromSerializedSnapshot(snapshot: SerializedSnapshot): TextBuffer {\n  1391→    const rid = replicaId(snapshot.replicaId);\n  1392→    const buffer = new TextBuffer(rid);\n  1393→\n  1394→    // Restore clock counter\n  1395→    buffer.clock.observe(snapshot.clockCounter - 1);\n  1396→\n  1397→    // Restore version vector\n  1398→    buffer._version = deserializeVersionVector(snapshot.versionVector);\n  1399→\n  1400→    // Restore fragments\n  1401→    const fragments: Fragment[] = snapshot.fragments.map((sf) => {\n  1402→      const insertionId = deserializeOperationId(sf.id);\n  1403→      const locator = deserializeLocator(sf.loc);\n  1404→      const baseLocator = deserializeLocator(sf.base);\n  1405→      const deletions = sf.del.map(deserializeOperationId);\n  1406→\n  1407→      return createFragment(insertionId, sf.io, locator, sf.t, sf.v, deletions, baseLocator);\n  1408→    });\n  1409→    buffer.fragments = SumTree.fromItems(fragments, fragmentSummaryOps);\n  1410→\n  1411→    // Restore undo map\n  1412→    buffer.undoMap.mergeFrom(\n  1413→      snapshot.undoMap.map((e) => ({\n  1414→        operationId: deserializeOperationId(e.op),\n  1415→        count: e.count,\n  1416→      })),\n  1417→    );\n  1418→\n  1419→    // Restore undo stack\n  1420→    buffer.undoStack = snapshot.undoStack.map((entry) => ({\n  1421→      transactionId: deserializeTransactionId(entry.txnId),\n  1422→      operationIds: entry.ops.map(deserializeOperationId),\n  1423→      undoCounts: entry.counts.map((c) => ({\n  1424→        operationId: deserializeOperationId(c.op),\n  1425→        oldCount: c.old,\n  1426→        newCount: c.new,\n  1427→      })),\n  1428→    }));\n  1429→\n  1430→    // Restore redo stack\n  1431→    buffer.redoStack = snapshot.redoStack.map((entry) => ({\n  1432→      transactionId: deserializeTransactionId(entry.txnId),\n  1433→      operationIds:
